event_preview:
Circuit de la Sarthe is a 13.629 km (8.469 mi) semi-permanent circuit built around public roads in Le Mans, France, incorporating the legendary Mulsanne Straight and the Porsche Curves. The 94th running of the 24 Hours of Le Mans takes place June 10-14, 2026, as the third round of the 2026 FIA World Endurance Championship. A 62-car entry spans three classes: 18 Hypercars, 19 LMP2 prototypes and 25 LMGT3 cars, with six additional cars on the reserve list.

Ferrari arrives as the defending race winner and defending double world champion, with Robert Kubica returning in the No. 83 AF Corse Ferrari seeking to repeat his 2025 victory. Toyota returns with its experienced GR010 Hybrid programme and two proven crews, while Cadillac brings three V-Series.R entries across Hertz Team JOTA and Wayne Taylor Racing. BMW M Team WRT fields two M Hybrid V8s, and Alpine continues with the A424. Genesis makes its Le Mans debut with two GMR-001 Hypercars, featuring André Lotterer alongside Pipo Derani and Mathys Jaubert. Porsche's absence from the Hypercar field — following its 2025 departure from the WEC — leaves space for eight manufacturers to battle for overall honours. Manthey Racing returns as the reigning LMGT3 champion, having won the inaugural LMGT3 races at Le Mans in both 2024 and 2025.

The all-time qualifying record at Circuit de la Sarthe belongs to Kamui Kobayashi, who set a 3:14.791 in the Toyota TS050 Hybrid LMP1 during the 2017 race — a mark unlikely to be challenged in the slower Hypercar era. The fastest race lap in the Hypercar class belongs to Kobayashi at 3:27.749.
